data.title: Lamborghini CEO says Trump tariffs causing billionaire customers to delay purchases, facing 27.5% import duties.
data.description: Lamborghini Automobili's CEO Stephan Winkelmann has revealed that due to the tariff uncertainty caused by President Donald Trump, even billionaire customers are delaying their purchases of supercars. The Italian luxury brand is currently facing 27.5% duties on exports to the U.S. Winkelmann stated that some wealthy clients are holding off on buying as they await confirmation on the final tariff figures. He noted, "They are millionaires or billionaires for a reason, so they know what they're doing." This information highlights the impact of trade policies on high-end automakers, demonstrating how economic uncertainties can influence consumer behavior and purchasing decisions.

data.title: Earnings reports for Frontline: $0.50 per share, $321.73 million revenue; LandBridge: $0.71 per share, $169.13 million revenue.
data.description: Before the market opens, several companies are scheduled to report their quarterly earnings. Frontline (NYSE: FRO) is anticipated to announce earnings of $0.50 per share with revenue expected to be around $321.73 million. LandBridge (NYSE: LB) is projected to report earnings of $0.71 per share with revenue estimated at $169.13 million.
